title: Minuartia subfalcata
taxonomy: Minuartia subfalcata belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a member of the genus Minuartia, which includes several species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Minuartia is further classified under the order Caryophyllales, which comprises various families of flowering plants. Minuartia subfalcata is native to Europe, where it can be found growing in rocky and grassy areas. As a member of the Caryophyllaceae family, Minuartia subfalcata shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as succulent leaves and small, white flowers.

intro: Minuartia subfalcata, commonly known as alpine pearlwort or short-leaved pearlwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Minuartia subfalcata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
